prompt: 2026: find the CLOSEST DIRECT competitors to kolm.ai (on-demand, reproducible, SIGNED + offline-verifiable security AUDIT of the AI an enterprise vendor ships, mapped to SOC 2/ISO 42001/NIST AI RMF/EU AI Act/OWASP/MITRE, that the buyer verifies themselves with no kolm account, free scan + $750 signed report + $299-$999/mo continuous + $15k/$25k tiers). Is there anyone doing SIGNED, buyer-verifiable AI security audits/attestations (e.g. Cyphrex, Sigstore-style attestations for AI, any 'AI BOM'/AI attestation startup)? For the COMPETITIVE MATRIX, list the exact comparison dimensions that matter to the buyer (price, lead time, on-demand vs scheduled, AI-agent-specific, signed + offline-verifiable, reproducible/continuous, buyer self-verifies with no account, framework mapping, the actual deliverable). For EACH category (GRC automation, questionnaire/trust center, pentest/PtaaS, AI security/red-team, traditional audit firm), give the one-line summary of price + what you get, and say where kolm uniquely wins and where a competitor is genuinely better. Be specific + cite.
content:
**No exact direct competitors match kolm.ai’s full profile** (on-demand/reproducible signed + offline-verifiable buyer-self-service AI security audits/attestations with no account required, free scan tier, specific multi-framework mapping including SOC 2/ISO 42001/NIST AI RMF/EU AI Act/OWASP/MITRE/LLM & Agentic Top 10, AI-agent-specific focus, and the pricing model of free scan + $750 signed report + subscription/enterprise tiers).[[1]](https://kolm.ai/changelog)

Closest elements appear in open-source/model provenance tools and certain AI security/compliance platforms that emphasize verifiable artifacts (e.g., AI BOMs or signed models), but they lack kolm’s combination of buyer-independent offline verification, on-demand signed reports, and the exact self-service/no-account model. Research prototypes (e.g., TEE-based attestable audits) exist but are not commercial products.[[2]](https://arxiv.org/html/2506.23706v1)

### Key Examples of Closest or Related Offerings
- **Sigstore/OpenSSF Model Signing (model-transparency project, v1.0+)**: Open-source library/CLI for cryptographically signing ML models (any format/size) using Sigstore (keyless via Fulcio/Rekor transparency logs), self-signed certs, or enterprise PKI. Produces verifiable sigstore bundles for provenance/integrity/tamper detection. Buyers verify offline with public tools. Adopted by NVIDIA (all NGC models since ~March 2025) and prototyped by Google/Kaggle. Free/open source. Focuses on model signing/supply-chain integrity rather than full security audits or broad framework mapping.[[3]](https://openssf.org/blog/2025/04/04/launch-of-model-signing-v1-0-openssf-ai-ml-working-group-secures-the-machine-learning-supply-chain/)
- **HiddenLayer**: AI security platform with model scanning, supply-chain verification, AI BOM (verifiable record of components/integrity), and runtime protections. Contributed to OpenSSF model signing. Produces machine-readable/auditable AI BOMs aligned with standards like OWASP CycloneDX. Enterprise platform (pricing not public; typical for such tools is subscription-based). Strong on verifiable records for governance but platform-centric rather than standalone signed/offline buyer reports.[[4]](https://www.hiddenlayer.com/insight/openssf-model-signing-for-safer-ai-supply-chains)
- **Mend.io**: Unified AppSec + AI security platform with continuous discovery/testing, AI-BOM + SBOM generation, red-teaming evidence, and mapping to EU AI Act, CRA, NIST, OWASP, ISO 42001. Emphasizes “verifiable proof”/audit-ready evidence and moving beyond self-attestation. Includes compliance posture reports and maturity assessments. Enterprise-focused (pricing not publicly detailed; one reference suggests tiers up to ~$300/user or custom). Strong continuous/compliance angle but not explicitly offline-verifiable signed reports or no-account buyer self-verification.[[5]](https://www.mend.io/)

Other mentions (e.g., Credo AI, Holistic AI for governance; various red-team/pentest tools) are further from the signed/offline/self-verifiable core. No prominent “Cyphrex” or similar standalone AI attestation startup matching the description was identified.

### Competitive Matrix Dimensions (Buyer-Relevant)
These are the factors that matter most for enterprise buyers seeking AI vendor audits/attestations:
- **Price**: Upfront/ongoing cost (free tiers, per-report, subscription, enterprise).
- **Lead time**: On-demand (minutes/hours) vs. scheduled (days/weeks).
- **On-demand vs. scheduled**: Self-service immediacy vs. booking/engagement required.
- **AI-agent-specific**: Tailored to agents/LLMs vs. general models or software.
- **Signed + offline-verifiable**: Cryptographic signatures allowing independent buyer verification (no vendor portal/account needed).
- **Reproducible/continuous**: Repeatable scans + ongoing monitoring vs. point-in-time.
- **Buyer self-verifies with no account**: Independent verification possible without creating a vendor account or sharing data.
- **Framework mapping**: Explicit coverage of SOC 2/ISO 42001/NIST AI RMF/EU AI Act/OWASP/MITRE/etc.
- **Actual deliverable**: What is received (e.g., signed PDF/report, BOM/attestation artifact, dashboard evidence, raw scan data).

### Category Summaries
**GRC automation** (e.g., Mend.io, Drata/Vanta-style tools, Credo AI): Automated evidence collection, continuous control monitoring, multi-framework mapping, and audit-ready packs/dashboards for SOC 2/ISO/etc. Pricing typically $10k–$100k+/year enterprise subscriptions. Get: Ongoing dashboards, evidence exports, and compliance posture reports.  
Kolm uniquely wins on AI-agent specificity, signed/offline-verifiable artifacts, buyer self-verification (no account), on-demand low-cost entry ($750 reports), and reproducibility. Competitors are genuinely better on broad enterprise GRC integration, non-AI scope, established audit workflows, and scale for non-AI compliance programs.[[6]](https://www.mend.io/compliance/)

**Questionnaire/trust center** (e.g., vendor risk platforms or self-serve trust centers): Scored questionnaires, trust center portals, and basic attestations (often self-reported). Pricing varies widely (free basic to subscription). Get: Questionnaires, basic reports, or portal access.  
Kolm wins on depth (actual technical signed audits vs. questionnaires), AI specificity, offline verifiability, and framework rigor. These are genuinely better/faster for lightweight vendor screening or basic transparency sharing.

**Pentest/PtaaS** (e.g., Cobalt, Bugcrowd, or similar platforms): Human or hybrid penetration testing with reports, often mapped to standards. Pricing $5k–$50k+ per engagement. Get: Detailed findings reports (sometimes signed by testers), remediation guidance. Typically scheduled.  
Kolm wins on speed (on-demand), price (especially free scan + $750), reproducibility/continuous options, AI-agent focus, and buyer-independent offline verification. Pentest platforms are genuinely better for deep human expertise, custom scoping, and recognized “human-signed” reports for certain auditors/regulators.[[7]](https://www.penligent.ai/hackinglabs/the-2026-ultimate-guide-to-ai-penetration-testing-the-era-of-agentic-red-teaming/)

**AI security/red-team** (e.g., HiddenLayer, Lakera, Calypso AI, or specialized red-team services): Model scanning, adversarial testing, runtime protections, and some AI BOMs or test reports. Pricing subscription or per-engagement (enterprise tiers common). Get: Scans, BOMs, test results, and platform dashboards.  
Kolm wins on signed/offline-verifiable deliverables, no-account buyer verification, explicit multi-framework mapping, on-demand accessibility, and the specific pricing transparency. These platforms are genuinely better on runtime/ongoing protection, broader AI threat coverage (beyond audits), and integrated developer workflows.[[8]](https://www.hiddenlayer.com/platform)

**Traditional audit firm** (e.g., Big 4 like Deloitte/PwC or specialized firms): Manual or hybrid audits with formal reports/attestations. Pricing $20k–$100k+ per audit. Get: Comprehensive human-led reports, often with firm branding/sign-off, suitable for regulatory submissions. Scheduled.  
Kolm wins on dramatically lower price, on-demand speed, reproducibility/continuous monitoring, AI specificity, and self-service/offline verification. Traditional firms are genuinely better on brand recognition, human judgment/expertise for complex or high-stakes audits, broader organizational scope, and perceived credibility with certain regulators or boards.

Overall, kolm’s differentiation centers on accessibility, cryptographic buyer empowerment, and AI-specific reproducibility at accessible pricing—areas where most alternatives remain either too platform-locked, manual/scheduled, or less verifiable independently. The space is evolving quickly with standards like OpenSSF model signing gaining traction.
